event: presented to emergency department with acute dyspnea
treated presumptively for health care–associated pneumonia
AV fistula creation in left forearm
tunneled right internal jugular vein hemodialysis catheter placement
differential diagnosis included severe sepsis due to pneumonia
acute coronary syndrome complicated by cardiogenic shock
electrocardiogram showed new lateral T-wave inversions
diagnosed with non-ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction
admitted to cardiac intensive care unit
right radial site used for arterial line
prior computed tomography angiography showed high-grade stenoses of bilateral common iliac arteries
coronary angiography via left radial arterial access through dormant AV fistula
left radial artery accessed using counterpuncture approach
6-F Glidesheath slender could not be fully advanced due to calcification
significant tortuosity in AV fistula prevented advancement
radial arteriogram demonstrated distal brachial artery occlusion
venous segment connected proximal radial artery to brachial artery
venous segment crossed with 0.014-inch Runthrough coronary wire
diagnostic catheters advanced through dormant fistula to coronary sinus
coronary angiography demonstrated heavily calcified 99% LAD and diagonal lesions
exchange for 6-F EBU4 guide catheter failed due to venous redundancies
BAT technique attempted with Euphora balloon
initial BAT failed due to guidewire kinking
attempts with 0.038-inch Amplatz Super Stiff guidewire failed
Resolute Onyx drug-eluting stent placed in LAD
Resolute Onyx stent placed in diagonal
